speaker: ROSALIND
text: O Jupiter, how weary are my spirits!

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: I care not for my spirits, if my legs were

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: not weary.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I could find in my heart to disgrace my

speaker: ROSALIND
text: man’s apparel and to cry like a woman, but I must

speaker: ROSALIND
text: comfort the weaker vessel, as doublet and hose

speaker: ROSALIND
text: ought to show itself courageous to petticoat. Therefore

speaker: ROSALIND
text: courage, good Aliena.

speaker: CELIA
text: I pray you bear with me. I cannot go no further.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: For my part, I had rather bear with you

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: than bear you. Yet I should bear no cross if I did

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: bear you, for I think you have no money in your

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: purse.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Well, this is the Forest of Arden.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Ay, now am I in Arden, the more fool I.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: When I was at home I was in a better place, but

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: travelers must be content.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Ay, be so, good Touchstone.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Look you who comes here, a young man and an old

speaker: ROSALIND
text: in solemn talk.

speaker: CORIN
text: That is the way to make her scorn you still.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: O Corin, that thou knew’st how I do love her!

speaker: CORIN
text: I partly guess, for I have loved ere now.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: No, Corin, being old, thou canst not guess,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Though in thy youth thou wast as true a lover

speaker: SILVIUS
text: As ever sighed upon a midnight pillow.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: But if thy love were ever like to mine—

speaker: SILVIUS
text: As sure I think did never man love so—

speaker: SILVIUS
text: How many actions most ridiculous

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Hast thou been drawn to by thy fantasy?

speaker: CORIN
text: Into a thousand that I have forgotten.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: O, thou didst then never love so heartily.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: If thou rememb’rest not the slightest folly

speaker: SILVIUS
text: That ever love did make thee run into,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Thou hast not loved.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Or if thou hast not sat as I do now,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Wearing thy hearer in thy mistress’ praise,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Thou hast not loved.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Or if thou hast not broke from company

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Abruptly, as my passion now makes me,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Thou hast not loved.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: O Phoebe, Phoebe, Phoebe!

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Alas, poor shepherd, searching of thy wound,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I have by hard adventure found mine own.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: And I mine. I remember when I was in

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: love I broke my sword upon a stone and bid him

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: take that for coming a-night to Jane Smile; and I

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: remember the kissing of her batler, and the cow’s

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: dugs that her pretty chopped hands had milked;

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: and I remember the wooing of a peascod instead of

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: her, from whom I took two cods and, giving her

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: them again, said with weeping tears “Wear these for

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: my sake.” We that are true lovers run into strange

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: capers. But as all is mortal in nature, so is all nature

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: in love mortal in folly.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Thou speak’st wiser than thou art ware of.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Nay, I shall ne’er be ware of mine own

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: wit till I break my shins against it.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Jove, Jove, this shepherd’s passion

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Is much upon my fashion.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: And mine, but it grows something stale

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: with me.

speaker: CELIA
text: I pray you, one of you question yond man, if he

speaker: CELIA
text: for gold will give us any food. I faint almost to death.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Holla, you clown!

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Peace, fool. He’s not thy kinsman.

speaker: CORIN
text: Who calls?

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Your betters, sir.

speaker: CORIN
text: Else are they very wretched.

speaker: ROSALIND
text:
Peace, I say.
                 
                
                  As
                   
                  Ganymede
                
                ,
                 
                
                  to
                   
                  Corin
                  .
                
               Good even to you, friend.

speaker: CORIN
text: And to you, gentle sir, and to you all.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I prithee, shepherd, if that love or gold

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Can in this desert place buy entertainment,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Bring us where we may rest ourselves and feed.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Here’s a young maid with travel much oppressed,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And faints for succor.

speaker: CORIN
text: Fair sir, I pity her

speaker: CORIN
text: And wish for her sake more than for mine own

speaker: CORIN
text: My fortunes were more able to relieve her.

speaker: CORIN
text: But I am shepherd to another man

speaker: CORIN
text: And do not shear the fleeces that I graze.

speaker: CORIN
text: My master is of churlish disposition

speaker: CORIN
text: And little recks to find the way to heaven

speaker: CORIN
text: By doing deeds of hospitality.

speaker: CORIN
text: Besides, his cote, his flocks, and bounds of feed

speaker: CORIN
text: Are now on sale, and at our sheepcote now,

speaker: CORIN
text: By reason of his absence, there is nothing

speaker: CORIN
text: That you will feed on. But what is, come see,

speaker: CORIN
text: And in my voice most welcome shall you be.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: What is he that shall buy his flock and pasture?

speaker: CORIN
text: That young swain that you saw here but erewhile,

speaker: CORIN
text: That little cares for buying anything.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I pray thee, if it stand with honesty,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Buy thou the cottage, pasture, and the flock,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And thou shalt have to pay for it of us.

speaker: CELIA
text: And we will mend thy wages. I like this place,

speaker: CELIA
text: And willingly could waste my time in it.

speaker: CORIN
text: Assuredly the thing is to be sold.

speaker: CORIN
text: Go with me. If you like upon report

speaker: CORIN
text: The soil, the profit, and this kind of life,

speaker: CORIN
text: I will your very faithful feeder be

speaker: CORIN
text: And buy it with your gold right suddenly.
